title: 127 games of dominance: The 20 wildest facts of SGA breaking Chamberlain's record
content: With Gilgeous-Alexander one game away from holding the most consecutive 20-point games in history, ESPN analyst Zach Kram breaks down the 20 most impressive stats about the streak.

title: LB Greenlaw returns to 49ers as DE Huff retires
content: Linebacker Dre Greenlaw is returning to the 49ers, agreeing to terms on a one-year, $7.5 million contract, a source told ESPN. Defensive end Bryce Huff, however, is retiring from the NFL, he announced.

title: Sources: Bengals add Allen in latest boost to D
content: Defensive tackle Jonathan Allen and the Bengals have agreed to a two-year, $28 million contract, sources told ESPN's Jeremy Fowler on Thursday.

title: Commanders add RB White to bolster backfield
content: The Commanders have agreed to terms with free agent running back Rachaad White, agent Sunny Shah told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Thursday.

title: DeRosa: 'New lease on life' for Team USA at WBC
content: Team USA manager Mark DeRosa says he's happy his squad has a 'new lease on life' at the World Baseball Classic after prematurely stating his team had reached the quarterfinals.

title: Evans: Niners were one piece away; I'm that guy
content: Veteran receiver Mike Evans said he ultimately signed with the 49ers in free agency because "they were one piece away ... and I think I am that piece."

title: Miami (OH) stunned by UMass, suffers 1st loss
content: No. 20 Miami (Ohio) suffered its first defeat with a shocking 87-83 loss to UMass in its first game of the Mid-American Conference tournament Thursday.

title: TTU's Anderson injured on Big 12's new glass floor
content: Texas Tech guard Christian Anderson strained a muscle while slipping on the new glass floor at the Big 12 tournament during Iowa State's 75-53 win over the Red Raiders on Thursday.

title: Trump: Iran should skip WC 'for their safety'
content: U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday that the Iran national team is "welcome" to compete at the FIFA World Cup in the United States, but advised it not to participate for "their own life and safety."

content: The prime minister also said the government is actively working on multiple fronts, and in the past few days, he spoke with the leaders of several countries about the developments in West Asia

title: Kerala nurses on the warpath over long-overdue wage revision
content: Hundreds of nurses working in private hospitals across Kerala are on an indefinite strike in pursuit of their demands, including a wage revision. Aathira Haridas listens to the grievances of the protesters

title: HC disqualification of SNDP Yogam director board a test of Kerala govt.’s mettle
content: Given Vellappally Natesan’s close ties with the ruling establishment, the Kerala government will have a tough task abiding by the High Court’s directive to temporarily take charge of the Yogam and to set up a provisional board to oversee affairs till elections are held

title: Behind the crumbling walls of Bihar’s Balirajgarh Fort, a tale of monumental neglect
content: The approval, issued by the ASI on February 26, is valid for a year and authorises the agency’s Patna Circle to undertake excavation at the site, officially listed as the ‘Remains of Ancient Fort of Garh’

title: LDF’s development narrative in Kollam constituency will face a stiff challenge from UDF
content: While the constituency has traditionally leaned towards the left, the Congress sprang a major surprise by winning Kollam Corporation in the recent local body elections. The BJP too has steadily raised its vote share

title: PM Modi speaks to Iranian President, discusses 'serious' situation in West Asia
content: The Prime Minister told Masoud Pezeshkian that the safety and security of Indian nationals, along with the need for unhindered transit of goods and energy, remain India's top priorities

title: Lacking long-term storage, India must rely on imports to manage LPG supply
content: In 2024-25, India’s LPG production was some 13 million tonnes as per government data; LPG consumption was around 31 million tonnes that year. Broadly, there is a 60-40 import-export split

title: India recieved requests for energy supplies from Bangladesh, Sri Lanka and Maldives, says MEA
content: India’s refining capacity, our own requirements, and diesel availability will be factored in while taking decisions, said MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal

title: Transgender people, men having sex with men, sex workers cannot donate blood, Centre insists in Supreme Court
content: Public health perspective must trump individual rights, says Ministry, noting that the right of the recipient to receive a safe blood transfusion far outweighs the right of an individual to donate blood

title: Inter departmental panel likely to check contamination in vegetables
content: Forest and Ecology Minister Eshwar Khandre says a meeting of Bengaluru legislators to be convened to discuss the issue shortly

title: Congress urges government to hold an all‑party meeting on women’s quota law
content: The government had reached out to at least two parties — the Congress and the Samajwadi Party — seeking their opinion on advancing the timeline for implementing the legislation

title: Railways launch drive against liquor sale in trains by aboard  staff
content: Action comes amid reports that housekeeping staff, pantry/catering and vendors were involved in illegal provision or sale of liquor, gutkha among other prohibited items on running trains

title: Reports of Iran allowing Indian ships through Strait of Hormuz ‘premature’: Centre
content: India has been expressing concern about the targeting of merchant navy ships that are stuck both inside the Gulf as well as in the Gulf of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz

title: Kochi Corporation to fast-track Konthuruthy compensation
content: Corporation secretary to initiate steps to distribute compensation to families residing on Konthuruthy river puramboke under the special rehabilitation package approved by the State Cabinet

title: No shortage of fuel in country, says government
content: There is no shortage of fuel and crude supply is secured, foremost priority of govt. is to provide cooking gas to households, regulation of commercial LPG will prevent black-marketing, says Petroleum Minister

title: Defence Forces Vision 2047 calls for space, cyber command and drone force
content: The strategic road map document, accessed by The Hindu, outlines broad goals that the armed forces must aspire to achieve as India prepares its military for evolving security challenges and the centenary of Independence

title: Supreme Court to look into what constitutes ‘personal data’ in DPDP laws
content: The DPDP laws in effect legalise disproportionate state surveillance, create a compensation vacuum for citizens, dilute the Right to Information, erode the ability of journalists to practice their profession, the petition said

title: India co-sponsors resolution passed by UNSC condemning Iran attacks on Gulf
content: India prioritises safety of ‘all civilians’ says MEA, but doesn’t criticise U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran and Lebanon

title: Parental income alone cannot decide OBC creamy layer status: Supreme Court
content: Rules in favour of children of senior PSU officials selected as OBC candidates in civil service exams; creamy layer exclusion framework says income from salaries, farm land cannot be included for wealth test

title: LPG shortage forces eateries to shut down, disrupts wedding catering in Bengaluru
content: The crisis has also begun to affect the city’s wedding catering industry, with several caterers halting new bookings and scaling down elaborate wedding menus

title: Supreme Court allows withdrawal of plea to bring back Netaji's mortal remains from Tokyo temple
content: The court said it would prefer to have Netaji’s sole heir and daughter, Anita Bose Pfaff, approach it with the prayer directly

title: No shortage of piped natural gas in Bengaluru: GAIL Gas
content: GAIL Gas spokesperson says hotels and eateries that are using commercial LPG currently and are concerned about supply can switch to PNG line subject to the availability within a matter of two months

title: West Asia conflict: Saudi oil tanker reaches Mumbai after crossing Strait of Hormuz
content: Shenlong, a tanker managed by Dynacom, sailed from Ras Tanura in Saudi Arabia and docked at the Mumbai port at 4.30 p.m. on March 11, according to a ship-tracking website

title: Madras High Court stays suit filed by PMK founder Ramadoss against his estranged son Anbumani
content: Justice T.V. Thamilselvi grants the interim stay till April 10, 2026 pursuant to a civil revision petition filed by party general secretary Vadivel Ravanan

title: Mamata plans protest against LPG, CNG crisis on March 16
content: Trinamool leadership criticises the Central government for its foreign policy decisions resulting in the rising fuel crisis

title: ISRO, AIIMS sign MoU for cooperation in space medicine and research
content: The memorandum aims to promote joint research aligned with ISRO’s priority areas, with a shared objective of advancing human health, performance and safety during human space missions, says the space agency

title: Nitish-led NDA government of 21 years failed in all parameters in Bihar, says Tejashwi Yadav
content: BJP leader Niraj Kumar hits back at the RJD leader, says NDA’s double-engine government in Bihar had pulled the State out of deterioration since 2005 and firmly placed it on the path to development

title: President’s secretariat declines TMC plea for appointment citing time constraints
content: The move comes days after President Droupadi Murmu expressed displeasure over arrangements during her West Bengal visit, triggering a political row

title: LPG shortage hits railway station kitchens; onboard catering continues
content: Hotel owners at the busy Krantivira Sangolli Rayanna Railway Station say the shortage has forced them to alter their menus and limit items that require continuous gas usage

title: LPG situation under control, no need for panic booking, says A.P. govt.
content: LPG stock of 17,962 MT available, steps being taken to ensure uninterrupted fuel supplies and protect households and priority sectors, announces govt.

title: Kharge blames Centre for ‘security collapse’ after attack on Farooq Abdullah
content: Congress president alleges plot to target senior leaders, says law and order has broken down since J&K lost Statehood; Nadda calls it a “murderous attack” but accuses Opposition of politicising the issue

title: CAPF officers demoralised that Centre is not giving them their due, Samajwadi MP tells Rajya Sabha
content: Calls for quick implementation of May 2025 SC order to grant Organised Group A Services status to CAPF officers, reduce IPS officers’ deputation in CAPFs; Centre’s review petition against ruling was rejected by the court

title: Thrissur achieves full housing coverage under LIFE Mission
content: District becomes first to ensure housing support for all eligible beneficiaries in the homeless-with-land category

title: 2,455 cases of atrocities against SCs, STs in Chhattisgarh since 2023 with nearly 40% instances of rape: government data
content: Apart from rapes and murders, there were 380 cases of causing hurt, 60 cases of causing grievous hurt and 30 cases of abduction, the Chhattisgarh government said in response to a query by a BJP MLA

title: ED attaches ₹581.65-crore assets in RHFL, RCFL case
content: Agency says cumulative attachment in Reliance Anil Ambani Group-linked cases now stands at ₹16,310 crore

title: LPG shortage hits operation of gas crematoria in Kozhikode, Kasaragod
content: Kozhikode Corporation Secretary has submitted a request to BPCL asking it to consider cremation as an essential service and address the shortage so that cremation services can be offered without interruption

title: Proposed NCB to  strengthen Kerala’s fight against drug menace
content: State Narcotic Control Bureau will function from the Excise headquarters with its units in all 14 districts; agency to have a cyber wing and divisions for research and analysis and operation

title: Tree felling banned in view of likely increase in demand for firewood
content: Forest Minister Eshwar Khandre asks officials to carry out inspection and launch vigilance activities to prevent tree felling

title: No disruption in supply of domestic LPG, says Mysuru DC
content: THE DC warned that strict legal action would be taken if anyone was found to be illegally hoarding LPG cylinders or selling them on the black market

title: Not stepped away from activism, it requires clarity and sincere dialogue: Sonam Wangchuk
content: The statement comes in the backdrop of an interview of his wife Gitanjali J. Angmo with The Hindu in which she remarked that Mr. Wangchuk will not pursue the path of agitation after he is released from detention

title: Income cannot be sole decider of OBC creamy layer: SC
content: The Supreme Court ruled that parental job status, not just income, must determine OBC 'creamy layer' exclusion. This decision impacts wards of PSU and private sector employees, aligning their criteria with government employees. The court emphasized that income alone is insufficient for exclusion, stressing the importance of job category and status.

title: Hours before Mojtaba Khamenei's 1st statement, reports claimed he was in coma
content: Reports suggest Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is critically wounded and in a coma following an air strike, possibly losing limbs and suffering severe internal injuries. This comes as Iranian state TV aired a statement vowing retaliation, while a government adviser claims he is "safe and sound.

title: H-1B visa employers database offline, key public records disappear; USCIS responds
content: The U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) H-1B Employer Data Hub has gone offline, disabling key search tools and removing recent datasets. This public database, launched in 2019, allowed users to analyze H-1B visa sponsorship data. USCIS has apologized for the technical difficulties and is working to restore service.

title: Shooting at US synagogue: Suspect dead; Trump terms attack 'terrible thing'
content: Police rushed to Temple Israel near Detroit following reports of an active shooter, with smoke billowing from the synagogue's roof. A truck reportedly crashed into the building, prompting a massive law enforcement response. Jewish institutions across the region were advised to enter a precautionary lockdown as authorities investigate the unfolding security incident.

title: Meta says it has removed over 150,000 Instagram, Facebook accounts, here's why
content: Meta has dismantled over 150,000 scam accounts across Facebook and Instagram, targeting criminal operations in Southeast Asia. This massive crackdown, a joint effort with international law enforcement, has led to 21 arrests. These sophisticated scam centers, operating like businesses, defrauded victims globally, highlighting the escalating fight against organized online fraud.

title: Need balance between privacy and public interest: SC
content: The Supreme Court stated that public interest cannot justify broad access to private information of public officials, emphasizing a balance with the right to privacy. The court will consider a petition challenging the Digital Personal Data Protection Act's restrictions, which a petitioner argued nullifies RTI provisions for accessing background data of appointees and could enable state surveillance.

title: Late Khamenei's wife alive; son recalls seeing Iran leader's body after US-Israel strike
content: Reports of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death in a US-Israel strike on February 28 have been officially denied by Iranian state media. This clarification follows a statement from Mojtaba Khamenei, who acknowledged his father's martyrdom and the deaths of other family members in the attack. The incident occurred during heightened regional tensions.

title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has a warning for all American technology companies
content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has warned tech companies about growing US criticism of AI, citing concerns over energy consumption and job cuts. He urged faster economic adoption of AI in America to maintain its global advantage, emphasizing its potential to improve the economy and rewrite societal rules. Recent polls indicate public sentiment leans towards concern over AI's risks.

title: NFL trade rumors: Packers, Jets & Steelers linked to $13M Ravens quarterback
content: The Baltimore Ravens recently released backup quarterback Cooper Rush. He was in the second year of his contract. This move opens doors for teams like the Green Bay Packers, New York Jets, Pittsburgh Steelers who are all seeking veteran presence in the QB room. With very few free agents remaining, Rush looks ideal for all three because of his strong gridiron presence. He filled in for Dak Prescott when he played for the Dallas Cowboys. Even Cowboys head coach speaks very highly of him.

title: Leanna Lenee and Travis Hunter divorce rumors: Did cheating really happen?
content: Travis Hunter and Leanna Lenee are happily married. Recent online rumors about their divorce and infidelity are false. These claims originated from parody accounts using manipulated videos. The couple tied the knot in May 2025 and welcomed a baby boy in August 2025. They are currently focused on their family and Travis's NFL career.

title: Bam Adebayo reportedly drug tested after record-breaking game against Wizards
content: Bam Adebayo stunned the NBA with an 83-point performance as the Miami Heat defeated the Washington Wizards 150-129. Viral social media posts claimed the National Basketball Association drug-tested or suspended him after the game, though the claims are false. Adebayo later reflected on surpassing Kobe Bryant’s 81-point mark, calling Bryant his childhood idol and saying the moment felt surreal.

title: Iran war economic impact: US, China, India - who are the winners and losers?
content: In a more pessimistic outlook outlined by Goldman Sachs, crude prices could climb back to around $100 per barrel and remain elevated. Such a development could reduce global economic growth by roughly half a percentage point while increasing inflation by nearly one percentage point over the next year.

title: 'Some people trying to create panic': PM Modi plays down LPG crisis; targets opposition
content: Prime Minister Modi downplayed the energy crisis, urging collective efforts and national interest over panic. He highlighted government initiatives like increased LPG connections and LNG terminals to boost self-reliance. Efforts are underway to shield citizens and farmers from the impact of global conflicts, with measures to manage LPG distribution and supply.

title: 'Strait of Hormuz will stay closed to pressure enemies', says Khamenei amid global fuel crisis
content: Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba  Khamenei vowed that Iran would continue fighting to avenge those killed in the conflict, including victims of attacks on civilian sites such as schools. Tehran, he said, would “obtain compensation” from its enemy in an apparent reference to the United States. If Washington refuses, Iran will “take from its assets” or destroy them to the same extent, he warned.

title: Priyadarshan admits mistake in portraying Shah Rukh Khan’s stardom in Billu
content: Filmmaker Priyadarshan reflected on his 2009 film 'Billu', admitting he added too many songs, which weakened its screenplay. He said portraying Shah Rukh Khan’s stardom needed no effort. Priyadarshan now reunites with Akshay Kumar for the horror-comedy Bhooth Bangla, releasing April 10, 2026, followed by Haiwaan with Saif Ali Khan.

title: Big shift in India’s job market! Why fixed term employment is the new normal
content: In simple terms, fixed term employment means hiring an employee directly on the employer’s payroll for a clearly defined period, backed by a written contract.

title: Pant undergoes special training with Yuvraj Singh ahead of IPL 2026 - Watch
content: Rishabh Pant, aiming to revive his white-ball career, underwent training sessions with 2011 World Cup hero Yuvraj Singh in Mumbai. This comes after an ordinary IPL 2025 season where Pant scored 269 runs, leading to Lucknow Super Giants finishing seventh. Pant hopes Yuvraj's guidance will help him turn around his fortunes ahead of IPL 2026.

title: US mulls suspending 'Jones Act' to curb oil prices amid Iran conflict - law explained
content: Amid escalating Iran conflict and rising oil prices, the Trump administration is considering a temporary waiver of the Jones Act. This maritime law requires US ships for domestic transport. The move aims to ensure free flow of vital energy and agricultural products to US ports by allowing foreign-flagged ships to operate domestically.

title: 'We make lot of money': Trump on rising oil prices amid US-Iran war
content: President Donald Trump said that US gains from higher oil prices. However, his main focus is stopping Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons. He described Iran as an "evil empire" whose nuclear ambitions threaten global stability. The conflict with Iran has entered its thirteenth day. The US has reportedly spent over $11.

title: US-Iran war: How Strait of Hormuz closure impacts global oil supply - explained in 5 charts
content: The Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy route, is effectively blocked due to the escalating US-Israeli war on Iran, halting tankers traffic and forcing oil producers to cut the oil output. Damaged infrastructure and blocked shipping lanes are causing global supply shocks, leading to surging energy prices and economic strain worldwide.

title: The billionaire whisperer: How Jeffrey Epstein built a fortune no one can fully explain
content: Jeffrey Epstein's vast wealth remains a puzzle. His financial career involved secretive deals and powerful connections. He earned millions from a few billionaire clients, including Les Wexner and Leon Black. Epstein also benefited from tax breaks in the US Virgin Islands. His empire eventually collapsed amid legal troubles. Investigations continue to uncover the full story of his fortune

title: Chinese man arrested for smuggling live ants in test tubes at Nairobi airport
content: Kenyan authorities apprehended a Chinese national at Nairobi's international airport. He was found with over 2,000 live queen ants hidden in his luggage. The insects were carefully packed to survive the journey. This discovery highlights a growing trade in rare insects. Officials are investigating potential links to a larger trafficking network.

title: Hundred Auction: Indian-owned SunRisers Leeds buy Pakistan player
content: Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed was signed by SunRisers Leeds for £190,000 at the Hundred Auction 2026, ending speculation that IPL-linked teams would avoid Pakistani players. Usman Tariq was earlier picked by Birmingham Phoenix. Meanwhile, Haris Rauf, Shadab Khan, and Saim Ayub went unsold, while Shaheen Afridi withdrew before the auction due to scheduling clashes.

title: LS showdown continues: Speaker stops Rahul mid-speech after his remark on Hardeep Puri
content: Lok Sabha witnessed a heated exchange as Rahul Gandhi's speech on the energy crisis was cut short by Speaker Om Birla. Gandhi was questioned for targeting Oil Minister Hardeep Singh Puri and deviating from the stated notice regarding gas shortages. The opposition had previously accused the Chair of limiting Gandhi's speaking time.

title: 'I don’t see a point': Pujara questions MS Dhoni’s batting position at CSK
content: MS Dhoni's batting position at CSK has sparked debate, with many believing he should bat higher to maximize his game-changing potential. Despite fitness concerns, his flashes of brilliance highlight his finishing ability. CSK CEO Kasi Viswanathan expressed confidence in Dhoni playing regularly, with the coaching staff to determine his exact role.

title: 'Purani Mandi, purana shehar, purana gaon': Kamal Singh harbored a 20-year grudge against Abdullah
content: A man identified as Kamal Singh Jamwal attempted to assassinate former Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Farooq Abdullah at a wedding in Jammu. Jamwal, who claimed to have planned the attack for 20 years, was apprehended by security personnel before he could fire. He stated he acted alone with his personal weapon, citing vague personal reasons for the attempt.

title: 'Simply not ready': US says its military cannot escort vessels in Hormuz right now
content: America's military is currently unable to escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated that military assets are focused on Iran's offensive capabilities. Escort operations are expected soon, possibly by month's end. US forces have eliminated Iranian vessels suspected of laying mines near the strait.

title: Rashmika Mandanna reacts as mother’s private chat goes viral after wedding
content: Rashmika Mandanna has threatened legal action after a private audio clip of her mother discussing a past engagement went viral. The actress stated that the deliberate circulation of the out-of-context conversation, eight years old, constitutes a serious invasion of privacy and crosses a line that cannot be ignored. She has demanded the immediate removal of the content within 24 hours.

title: WC controversy: Pakistan's Mirza denies 'misbehaviour’, sends legal notice
content: Pakistan pacer Salman Mirza sent a legal notice to a news outlet and journalist over reports alleging he misbehaved with a woman during the T20 World Cup 2026 in Sri Lanka. Calling the claims “false, malicious, and highly defamatory,” Mirza denied wrongdoing. The PCB also dismissed the reports, demanding a public apology and warning of action.

title: Anurag Kashyap reacts to brother Abhinav Kashyap’s remarks on Salman Khan
content: Filmmaker Anurag Kashyap has broken his silence on his brother Abhinav Kashyap's public spat with Salman Khan. Anurag stated he respects his brother's request not to comment publicly, despite the ongoing defamation case filed by the Khan brothers against Abhinav. Abhinav had made strong criticisms against Salman and Arbaaz Khan, leading to the legal action.

title: Watch: Moment Iranian 'suicide boat' struck US-owned tanker
content: Iran's attack on the US-owned tanker 'Safesea Vishnu' near Basra has claimed the life of an Indian national, with 27 others rescued. This incident, occurring amidst escalating West Asia tensions, has led to the suspension of oil terminal operations in Iraq. India's External Affairs Ministry is prioritizing the welfare of its diaspora and coordinating assistance for stranded nationals.

title: 'He's like Messi or Ronaldo': Ex-England captain's jaw-dropping comparison for Bumrah
content: Michael Vaughan hailed Jasprit Bumrah as the finest bowler he's ever witnessed after his T20 World Cup final heroics.  Vaughan compared Bumrah's impact to football legends Messi and Ronaldo, stating his presence guarantees victory.  Fellow experts agreed, though Alastair Cook questioned his iconic Test spells, a point Vaughan countered with Bumrah's impressive overseas Test records.

title: 'Jaishankar spoke to Iran FM 3 times': India flags ships safety, energy security concerns
content: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar held three recent talks with his Iranian counterpart, focusing on maritime shipping safety and India's energy security amid rising Middle East tensions. Iran has ensured safe passage for Indian-flagged vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial route for global oil trade and India's energy imports.

title: $256K in, what’s next for Nepal? Competitive cricket, infra boost and a hand from India
content: In the past two editions of the T20 World Cups, Nepal's fans have flooded the stands, be it in Texas or in Mumbai. The cricketers have shown the spunk, stomach for a fight, and pushed teams like South Africa and England to the brink. The Rohit Paudel-led team registered their first World Cup win in their third appearance, when they defeated Scotland at Wankhede. Now they must aim higher, but to achieve the summit of Mount Everest, they will need help.

title: "Will Do Job On Ground": Netanyahu Warns Lebanon To Act Against Hezbollah
content: Israel renewed its strikes on Beirut on Thursday, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warning that if the Lebanese government did not disarm Hezbollah, Israel would do the job "on the ground".

title: Iran-Israel War LIVE Updates: Netanyahu Says Top Iranian Nuclear Scientists Killed In Israeli Strikes
content: US-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Iran has allowed India-flagged tankers to pass through the Strait of Hormuz following talks between External Affairs Minister (EAM) S Jaishankar and his Iranian counterpart, Abbas Araghchi.

title: Netanyahu Says Top Iranian Nuclear Scientists Killed In Israeli Strikes
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says Israeli attacks have killed top Iranian nuclear scientists.

title: NASA Says "On Track" For Artemis 2 Launch As Soon As April 1
content: NASA said Thursday that the long-delayed launch of the Artemis 2 mission, part of the US push to return to the Moon by 2028, could come as soon as April 1.

title: Delhi Metro Enthusiast Felicitated For Riding First Train On All New Corridors
content: Union Minister of State for Housing and Urban Affairs Tokhan Sahu felicitated Anil Marwah, a dedicated enthusiast of the Delhi Metro, for his personal milestone of riding the first train on the opening day of every newly inaugurated metro corridor.

title: Armed Man Rams Vehicle Into Michigan Synagogue, Shot Dead By Security
content: A man armed with a rifle rammed a vehicle into the nation's largest Reform synagogue, in a Detroit suburb, and was fatally shot by security, The Associated Presshas reported.

title: Delhi Rolls Out GPS-Tracked Tankers To Ensure Transparent Water Supply
content: The Delhi government has introduced GPS-enabled tracking and a dedicated mobile app for water tankers as part of its Summer Action Plan 2026.

title: "It Is Like Auctions": Electric Stove Demand Goes Through The Roof Amid LPG Crisis
content: Storekeepers say that sales that would normally take a month have been completed within just three days, leaving many outlets with empty shelves and forcing buyers to compete with each other for the remaining stock.

title: APJ Abdul Kalam Foresaw This: Gulf War's Hard Lesson On Energy Imports
content: Twenty years ago President APJ Abdul Kalam, the 'Missile Man' sought India to be energy independent, today his dream remains unfulfilled

title: Ground Report: Ops Normal At Israel's Haifa Port Amid Iran Attack Rumours
content: Social media has been flooded with claims that Haifa Port in Israel run by the Adani Group has come under missile attack from Iran or Hezbollah.

title: Madhya Pradesh To Tag 40 Lakh Stray Cattle With Saffron Tags
content: In a major step aimed at tackling the growing menace of stray cattle on roads and farms, Madhya Pradesh's proposal to tag nearly 40 lakh stray cattle with unique 12-digit saffron identification tags has received in-principle approval from the Centre.

title: From Ground To Aviation: Why Hormuz Disruption Is Making Every Fuel Pricier
content: The war in the Middle East is snarling one of the world's most critical energy arteries, and the ripple effects are global. The Strait of Hormuz, the narrow passage between Iran and Oman, normally carries about 20 million barrels of oil per day.

title: Woman Drowns 4 Daughters, Then Dies By Suicide In Madhya Pradesh
content: A woman from Madhya Pradesh's Sagar district allegedly threw her four minor daughters into a well before committing suicide by hanging herself at her home.

title: At 17, Mojtaba Khamenei Built Network That Made Him Iran's Supreme Leader
content: On March 8 2026, ten days after US-Israeli strikes killed his father, Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed Iran's third supreme leader. At 56, he became the most powerful figure in a country of 90 million people, at war and under siege.

title: Iran's Historic Palaces, UNESCO Sites Damaged In US-Israeli Bombing
content: US and Israeli strikes on Iran have damaged four cultural and historical sites, including palaces and an ancient mosque.

title: "Those Trying To Create Panic Over LPG Are Hurting The Country": PM Modi
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday hit out at those trying to create panic on the LPG situation in India, and said they are not only exposing themselves before the people but also hurting the country.

title: How A Family Dispute Is Shadowing Jaswant Singh Legacy In Rajasthan
content: This family drama has gripped western Rajasthan, as it centers on the political legacy of Jaswant Singh, a nine-time MP and towering figure who served as India's Foreign and Finance Minister.

title: Adani Foundation To Connect 10 Lakh Women Under Swambhimaan Initiative: Priti Adani
content: The Adani Foundation, today, declared that in the next one year, it will connect one lakh women in Maharashtra with the Swabhimaan initiative.

title: Iran's "Hunt Down US Servicemen" Warning If Trump Attacks Electricity
content: Iran's security chief, Ali Larijani, threatened to target power grids in the region if the United States attacked the Islamic republic's supplies.

title: "We Are Not Ready" To Escort Tankers Through Strait Of Hormuz, Says US
content: The US military is currently "not ready" to escort tankers through the critical Strait of Hormuz because all its assets are focused on striking Iran, Energy Secretary Chris Wright said Thursday.

title: UCL talking points: Spurs awful as Premier League teams slump
content: arsenal drew 1-1 with bayer leverkusen in the UEFA champions league round of 16 . but some of the competition heavyweights are starting to make some noise . ESPN FC writers Mark Ogden, Sam Tighe, Gab Marcotti and Alex Kirkland are here to break down the action as it happened .

content: Bharti Jain is senior editor with The Times of India, New Delhi. She has been writing on security matters since 1996. Having covered the Union home ministry, security agencies, Election Commission and the ‘prime’ political beat, the Congress, for The Economic Times all these years, she moved to TOI in August 2012.
